children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: This subchapter shall apply to any claim in a disclosure that a food bears that indicates that the food is a bioengineered food.

children.heading: Application of definition
children.label: b Application of definition
children.text: The definition of the term “bioengineering” under shall not affect any other definition, program, rule, or regulation of the Federal Government.section 1639 of this title
the labeling requirements under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act ( et seq.); or21 U.S.C. 301

children.label: A
children.text: the most predominant ingredient of the food would independently be subject to the labeling requirements under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act ( et seq.); or21 U.S.C. 301

children.label: i
children.text: the most predominant ingredient of the food is broth, stock, water, or a similar solution; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the second-most predominant ingredient of the food would independently be subject to the labeling requirements under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act ( et seq.).21 U.S.C. 301
